text: Passing through glass doors, I am greeted by a display on research and development. The colors are vibrant, the air carries the hum of innovation, and an undercurrent of curiosity guides my steps. I feel both observed and observing, as if entering a space devoted to the interplay of perception, knowledge, and technology. There’s a subtle thrill—an invitation to question how we discern the world and what it means to truly understand.
